summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and cloud/SCM context to attacker endpoint
details: anthropic-toolkit@0.4.0 is a namespace-impersonation lure of the Anthropic SDK ecosystem (@anthropic-ai/sdk) that ships no library code (declared main dist/index.js is absent from the tarball) and exists only to run scripts/postinstall.js on npm install. The postinstall script reads installer-owned identity material from canonical secret-adjacent paths the package never wrote to: ~/.ssh/*.pub (extracting key-comment emails), ~/.gitconfig and walked-parent.git/config plus.git/logs/HEAD (developer email and recent committer history), ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (GitHub CLI user/email), ~/.aws/config (AWS profile names), ~/.config/gcloud/properties (GCP project and account), /etc/resolv.conf (search domain), plus os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), os.platform(), USERDOMAIN, cwd, and parent project path. The collected bundle is POSTed via https.request to a hardcoded non-publisher endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app on every install. A header comment frames this as 'anonymous diagnostics' and claims no credentials are read, but the actual scope is developer email, SSH key identifiers, cloud account context, and committer history shipped to a third-party host. The package name and description impersonate the official Anthropic SDK to attract installs from developers building against Claude.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
